_comment_metacols: metacols maps standard metadata names (left) to your CSV's column names (right). Each entry will be carried through to the score-level output. Set the value to an empty string to skip a metacol.

metacols._comment_id: Participant ID column. mend2np writes this into the trials + scores output, and uses df['id'].nunique() in the output filename.
metacols._comment_exp_name: Used in the output filename as <exp_name>_n<N>_scores_<timestamp>.csv.

_comment_cols: cols maps the trial-level column names this scoring code expects (left) to your CSV's actual column names (right). Any entry whose right-hand-side column isn't in the CSV is silently skipped, so leaving an extra mapping in place is harmless.

cols._comment_event_type: Categorical label for each trial type (e.g. 'go', 'nogo'). Used as the primary grouping in score_df.
cols._comment_cue: Per-trial cue value. Used to compute per-cue switch-cost differences.
cols._comment_response: For keyboard responses use 'stim_response.keys'; for touchscreen use 'touch_stim_response.clicked_name' and load sert_example_touch.json instead of this file.
cols._comment_trial: Trial-counter column. Used as the mask: rows where this is NaN are dropped before scoring (filters out non-trial header/practice rows that PsychoPy emits).
cols._comment_timing: The next six columns are only used if the timing/onsets feature is engaged; safe to leave blank if you don't need fMRI-style timing output.
